    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html lang="en">
    <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Patagonia 2026 — Trip Dashboard</title>
    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&family=DM+Mono:wght@300;400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
    :root {
    --ink: #1a1a18;
    --paper: #f5f0e8;
    --muted: #8a8070;
    --accent: #2a5c45;
    --accent2: #c4773b;
    --ship: #1e3a5f;
    --warm: #e8dcc8;
    --border: #d4c9b0;
    }
    * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
    body { background: var(--paper); color: var(--ink); font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.6; }
    .header { background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper); padding: 40px 28px 28px; position: relative; overflow: hidden; }
    .header::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: -40px; right: -40px; width: 260px; height: 260px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--accent); opacity: 0.15; }
    .header-eyebrow { font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.25em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent2); margin-bottom: 8px; }
    .header h1 { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-size: clamp(26px, 6vw, 48px); font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.1; margin-bottom: 6px; }
    .header h1 em { font-style: italic; color: #a8c5b5; }
    .header-sub { color: #9a9080; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; }
    .countdown-badge { margin-top: 16px; display: inline-block; background: var(--accent2); color: white; padding: 6px 16px; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; }
    .tabs { display: flex; background: var(--warm); border-bottom: 2px solid var(--border); overflow-x: auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch; }
    .tab { padding: 14px 18px; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; color: var(--muted); background: none; border: none; border-bottom: 3px solid transparent; font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace; transition: all 0.2s; }
    .tab:hover { color: var(--ink); }
    .tab.active { color: var(--accent); border-bottom: 3px solid var(--accent); background: var(--paper); }
    .section { display: none; padding: 24px 20px 60px; max-width: 680px; }
    .section.active { display: block; }
    .section-title { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 400; margin-bottom: 4px; }
    .section-subtitle { font-size: 12px; color: var(--muted); letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 24px; }
    /* ITINERARY */
    .itinerary-day { border-left: 2px solid var(--border); padding: 0 0 24px 18px; position: relative; margin-left: 38px; }
    .itinerary-day:last-child { padding-bottom: 0; }
    .day-dot { position: absolute; left: -7px; top: 4px; width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--paper); border: 2px solid var(--muted); }
    .itinerary-day.highlight .day-dot { background: var(--accent); border-color: var(--accent); }
    .itinerary-day.ship-day .day-dot { background: var(--ship); border-color: var(--ship); }
    .itinerary-day.highlight { border-left-color: var(--accent); }
    .itinerary-day.ship-day { border-left-color: var(--ship); }
    .itinerary-day.transit-day .day-dot { background: var(--accent2); border-color: var(--accent2); }
    .day-label { position: absolute; left: -48px; top: 0; font-size: 12px; color: var(--muted); text-align: right; width: 30px; }
    .day-date { font-size: 12px; color: var(--muted); letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 2px; }
    .day-title { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 3px; }
    .day-hotel { font-size: 13px; color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 5px; }
    .day-detail { font-size: 14px; color: #5a5040; line-height: 1.6; }
    .tag { display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 2px; margin-bottom: 5px; }
    .tag-ship { background: #dce8f5; color: var(--ship); }
    .tag-land { background: #dceee6; color: var(--accent); }
    .tag-transit { background: #fdeede; color: var(--accent2); }
    .alert-inline { font-size: 13px; color: #8a4010; background: #fdf4e8; border-left: 2px solid var(--accent2); padding: 6px 10px; margin-top: 5px; }
    /* PACKING */
    .pack-category { margin-bottom: 24px; }
    .pack-category h3 { font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); padding-bottom: 5px; margin-bottom: 10px; }
    .pack-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; padding: 6px 0; cursor: pointer; }
    .pack-item input[type="checkbox"] { margin-top: 3px; accent-color: var(--accent); width: 16px; height: 16px; flex-shrink: 0; cursor: pointer; }
    .pack-item label { cursor: pointer; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; }
    .pack-item input:checked + label { text-decoration: line-through; color: var(--muted); }
    .pack-note { font-size: 12px; color: var(--accent2); display: block; margin-top: 2px; }
    .pack-progress { background: var(--warm); border-radius: 2px; height: 5px; margin-bottom: 20px; overflow: hidden; }
    .pack-progress-bar { background: var(--accent); height: 100%; width: 0%; transition: width 0.3s; border-radius: 2px; }
    .pack-count { font-size: 13px; color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 8px; }
    /* MEDICATIONS */
    .med-card { border: 1px solid var(--border); padding: 14px; margin-bottom: 14px; background: white; }
    .med-name { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 3px; }
    .med-type { display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 3px 9px; margin-bottom: 8px; }
    .med-type.seasick { background: #dce8f5; color: var(--ship); }
    .med-type.gi { background: #dceee6; color: var(--accent); }
    .med-type.pain { background: #fdeede; color: var(--accent2); }
    .med-type.antibiotic { background: #f0dcea; color: #6a3060; }
    .med-type.kit { background: var(--warm); color: var(--muted); }
    .med-timing { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--ink); margin-bottom: 4px; }
    .med-detail { font-size: 13px; color: #5a5040; line-height: 1.6; }
    .med-note { margin-top: 8px; padding: 8px; background: #fdf8f0; border-left: 2px solid var(--accent2); font-size: 12px; color: #8a5020; }
    .seasick-ladder { background: var(--warm); border: 1px solid var(--border); padding: 14px; margin-bottom: 20px; }
    .seasick-ladder h3 { font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 10px; }
    .ladder-step { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: flex-start; margin-bottom: 8px; font-size: 13px; }
    .ladder-num { background: var(--ship); color: white; width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 11px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; }
    /* NEED TO KNOW */
    .info-block { border: 1px solid var(--border); padding: 14px; margin-bottom: 14px; background: white; }
    .info-block h3 { font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 10px; }
    .info-block p, .info-block li { font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.7; color: #3a3028; }
    .info-block ul { padding-left: 16px; }
    .info-block li { margin-bottom: 5px; }
    .alert-block { background: #fdf4e8; border-left: 3px solid var(--accent2); padding: 12px 14px; margin-bottom: 14px; font-size: 13px; color: #7a4010; line-height: 1.6; }
    .alert-block strong { display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; }
    @media (max-width: 480px) {
    .header { padding: 28px 18px 22px; }
    .section { padding: 18px 16px 50px; }
    .itinerary-day { margin-left: 34px; }
    }
    </style>
    </head>
    <body>
    <div class="header">
    <div class="header-eyebrow">OAT · Apr 2 – Apr 20, 2026 · 19 Days</div>
    <h1>The <em>Wilderness</em><br>Beyond</h1>
    <div class="header-sub">Patagonia · Tierra del Fuego · Chilean Fjords · Iguassu Falls</div>
    <div class="countdown-badge" id="countdown">🧭 Loading...</div>
    <div style="margin-top:10px;"><a href="emergency-doc.html" style="color:#a8c5b5;font-size:10px;letter-spacing:0.15em;text-transform:uppercase;text-decoration:none;">📄 Emergency Document →</a></div>
    </div>
    <div class="tabs">
    <button class="tab active" onclick="showTab('itinerary')">Itinerary</button>
    <button class="tab" onclick="showTab('packing')">Packing</button>
    <button class="tab" onclick="showTab('meds')">Medications</button>
    <button class="tab" onclick="showTab('need-to-know')">Need to Know</button>
    <button class="tab" onclick="showTab('culture')">Culture & Places</button>
    <button class="tab" onclick="showTab('route-map')">Route Map</button>
    </div>
    <!-- ITINERARY -->
    <div id="itinerary" class="section active">
    <div class="section-title">Day by Day</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">Main trip + post-trip extension</div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D1</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 2, Thu</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">Depart</span>
    <div class="day-title">Depart U.S. → Buenos Aires</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Seattle → JFK: Alaska AS026, departs 10:44am, arrives 7:06pm (Terminal 8)<br>JFK → Buenos Aires: American AA953, departs 11:20pm, arrives Apr 3 10:55am (Terminal 8 · Seat 29C · 1 bag)<br>💊 Take melatonin + magnesium glycinate ~30min before you want to sleep on the overnight leg</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D2</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 3, Fri</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Buenos Aires</span>
    <div class="day-title">Arrive Buenos Aires</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Recoleta Grand Hotel (2 nights)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">OAT rep meets you at airport · Tango lesson · Orientation walk · Recoleta neighborhood<br>Meals: Dinner on own</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D3</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 4, Sat</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Buenos Aires</span>
    <div class="day-title">Explore Buenos Aires</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Recoleta Grand Hotel</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Bus tour · Plaza de Mayo · Casa Rosada · La Boca · "Dirty War" discussion at café · Afternoon free — optional visit to Evita Museum (Eva Duarte's role as first lady) · Home-hosted dinner<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Dinner (home-hosted)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D4</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 5, Sun</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">Internal Flight</span>
    <div class="day-title">Fly to Ushuaia</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Cilene del Faro Hotel</div>
    <div class="day-detail">City at the End of the World · Museo del Fin del Mundo · Prison Museum · Dinner with local family<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Dinner (hosted)</div>
    <div class="alert-inline">⚠️ Take Bonine tonight before bed — sea conditions ahead</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day ship-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D5</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 6, Mon</div>
    <span class="tag tag-ship">Board Ship</span>
    <div class="day-title">Ushuaia → Embark Ventus Australis</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🚢 M/V Ventus Australis (4 nights)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Morning free in Ushuaia · Falklands War veteran discussion · Board ship late afternoon · Safety drill · Captain's Welcome Dinner<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own), Dinner (ship)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day ship-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D6</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 7, Tue</div>
    <span class="tag tag-ship">At Sea</span>
    <div class="day-title">Cape Horn · Wulaia Bay</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🚢 M/V Ventus Australis</div>
    <div class="day-detail">⚠️ Pre-dawn Zodiac to Cape Horn (weather permitting) — 160 steps, 2-mile walk, extremely rough seas possible · ⭐ The Ventus Australis is the only ship permitted to land on Cape Horn · Wulaia Bay hike afternoon — 3-mile uphill, steep & muddy<br>💊 Seasickness: Stay on deck in fresh air, keep eyes on horizon, eat light but not empty. Going below to your cabin makes it much worse.<br>Meals: All onboard (breakfast after excursion)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day ship-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D7</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 8, Wed</div>
    <span class="tag tag-ship">At Sea</span>
    <div class="day-title">Pia Glacier · Porter Glacier</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🚢 M/V Ventus Australis</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Zodiac to Pia Glacier · Beagle Channel · Porter Glacier Zodiac · Magellan Strait presentation · Demanding afternoon hike<br>Meals: All onboard</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day ship-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D8</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 9, Thu</div>
    <span class="tag tag-ship">At Sea</span>
    <div class="day-title">Agostini Sound · Águila & Condor Glaciers</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🚢 M/V Ventus Australis</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Darwin Cordillera · Two Zodiac excursions · Watch for Andean Condors · Captain's Farewell Dinner<br>Meals: All onboard</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D9</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 10, Fri</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Puerto Natales</span>
    <div class="day-title">Magdalena Island · Disembark · Puerto Natales</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Hotel Costaustralis</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Early morning Zodiac to Magdalena Island — 120,000 penguins! · Disembark Punta Arenas · ~3.5hr bus to Puerto Natales<br>Meals: Breakfast (ship), Lunch (boxed), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    <div class="alert-inline">⚠️ April departure: penguin viewing replaced with sea lion Zodiac at Marta Island</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D10</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 11, Sat</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Torres del Paine</span>
    <div class="day-title">Torres del Paine · Horseback Riding</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Hotel Lago Grey (2 nights)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">UNESCO World Biosphere Reserve · Horseback ride ~1hr · Afternoon scenic hike with local guide · Paine Massif · Watch for guanaco, puma, condors<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (boxed), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D11</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 12, Sun</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Torres del Paine</span>
    <div class="day-title">Torres del Paine · Grey Glacier</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Hotel Lago Grey</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Morning hike to Salto Grande beach · Watch for guanaco, ñandú, condors · Optional afternoon hike to Grey Lake and 4-mile-wide Grey Glacier<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (boxed), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D12</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 13, Mon</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">Long Drive</span>
    <div class="day-title">Torres del Paine → El Calafate</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Hotel Kosten Aike (2 nights)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">⚠️ 10-12 hour bus transfer · Final photo stop at Torres del Paine before leaving the park · Border crossing Argentina/Chile — passport required, can take hours · Sheep ranch visit + shearing demo + BBQ lunch en route · Arrive El Calafate late afternoon<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (ranch BBQ), Dinner (own)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D13</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 14, Tue</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">El Calafate</span>
    <div class="day-title">Los Glaciares NP · Perito Moreno Glacier</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Hotel Kosten Aike</div>
    <div class="day-detail">UNESCO World Heritage Site · Perito Moreno Glacier — 200ft tall! Watch for calving · Catwalk walks over ice · Optional: La Tienda de Vinos wine tasting ($41)<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (boxed), Dinner (restaurant)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D14</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 15, Wed</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">Internal Flight</span>
    <div class="day-title">El Calafate → Buenos Aires</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Recoleta Grand Hotel</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Morning flight to Buenos Aires · Farewell Dinner at local restaurant<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own), Dinner (farewell, included)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D15</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 16, Thu</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Buenos Aires</span>
    <div class="day-title">Buenos Aires · Recoleta Cemetery · Paraná Delta</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Recoleta Grand Hotel (post-trip)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Eva Perón's grave at Recoleta Cemetery · Boat ride in Paraná Delta · Free afternoon — optional: Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes (10 min walk, free, Goya/Van Gogh/Gauguin) or Plazoleta Paseo de la Recoleta · ⚠️ Pack a carry-on sized bag for 4 nights at Iguassu — leave main checked luggage at hotel in Buenos Aires. You'll pick it up on return Apr 20 before flying home.<br>What goes in the Iguassu bag: 4 nights light clothing (90°F+), swimsuit, toiletries, all medications, Brazil visa printout, yellow fever card, passport, permethrin-treated clothes, mosquito repellent, camera.<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D16</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 17, Fri</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">POST-TRIP · Fly</span>
    <div class="day-title">Fly to Iguassu Falls · Brazilian Side</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Amerian Portal del Iguazu (3 nights)</div>
    <div class="day-detail">⚠️ Store larger luggage at airport on arrival — you go straight to the falls before checking in · ⚠️ Brazil border crossing — have visa printout ready · Brazilian side of falls — massive spray, rainbow views · Welcome Dinner<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own after border), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    <div class="alert-inline">⚠️ 90°F+ with 90% humidity — pack accordingly. Yellow fever card + Brazil visa required.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D17</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 18, Sat</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Iguassu Falls</span>
    <div class="day-title">Guaraní Community · Biocentro</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Amerian Portal del Iguazu</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Jungle hike to Guaraní community · Medicinal herbs, crafts, culture · Biocentro — butterfly display, reptiles · Afternoon free (pool, hummingbird garden, market)<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (Biocentro), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day highlight">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D18</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 19, Sun</div>
    <span class="tag tag-land">Iguassu Falls</span>
    <div class="day-title">Argentinean Side of Falls · Devil's Throat</div>
    <div class="day-hotel">🏨 Amerian Portal del Iguazu</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Narrow-gauge train to Devil's Throat — 230ft plunge · Upper Circuit catwalk trail · Optional: Great Adventure boat tour ($75) — jungle + rapids + waterfall<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own), Dinner (hotel)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="itinerary-day transit-day">
    <div class="day-dot"></div>
    <div class="day-label">D19</div>
    <div class="day-date">Apr 20, Mon</div>
    <span class="tag tag-transit">Depart</span>
    <div class="day-title">Depart Buenos Aires → Home</div>
    <div class="day-detail">Buenos Aires → JFK: American AA954, departs 9:10pm, arrives Apr 21 6:55am (Terminal 8 · Seat 28J · 1 bag)<br>JFK → Seattle: Alaska AS023, departs 11:30am, arrives 2:50pm<br>✅ Global Entry — clears customs fast, plenty of time for the 11:30am connection<br>Meals: Breakfast (hotel), Lunch (own)</div>
    </div>
    </div>
    <!-- PACKING -->
    <div id="packing" class="section">
    <div class="section-title">Packing List</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">Check items off as you pack</div>
    <div class="pack-count" id="pack-count">0 of 0 packed</div>
    <div class="pack-progress"><div class="pack-progress-bar" id="pack-bar"></div></div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>⚠️ Before You Leave (Do These First)</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0a"><label for="p0a">Spray clothes with Permethrin (for Brazil leg) — do 24–48hrs before departure, let dry completely</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0b"><label for="p0b">Apply for / confirm Brazil e-visa is printed and saved digitally</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0c"><label for="p0c">Passport valid 6+ months past Apr 20 return date</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0c2"><label for="p0c2">Add Global Entry KTN to American Airlines profile <span class="pack-note">So it appears on your boarding pass — aa.com → your account → travel info</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0d"><label for="p0d">Yellow fever exemption card in wallet</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0e"><label for="p0e">⚠️ Call OAT to confirm travel insurance — 1-800-221-0814 <span class="pack-note">Confirm $75K evac coverage satisfies their $200K requirement. Plan #UF694B.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0ins"><label for="p0ins">FootprintID profile — already set up ✓ <span class="pack-note">tripmate.footprintid.com · Membership #80-331-751 · Access Code 1183</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0f"><label for="p0f">Print emergency document (passport copy, card numbers, contacts) <span class="pack-note">Store separately from originals — one copy in daypack, one in checked bag</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0ins2"><label for="p0ins2">Screenshot TripMate insurance info to phone <span class="pack-note">Plan #UF694B · Emergency: +1-954-334-8953 (collect outside US)</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0fid"><label for="p0fid">Put FootprintID card in wallet <span class="pack-note">The blue card — Membership #80-331-751 · Emergency: +1-855-374-3411</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0g"><label for="p0g">Save emergency document to phone Notes <span class="pack-note">This is your backup if you lose the printed copy</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0print"><label for="p0print">⚠️ Print the emergency document <span class="pack-note">No internet or cell service on the ship (Apr 6–9) or in remote Patagonia — printed copy is essential</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p0offline"><label for="p0offline">Save this dashboard for offline use on your phone <span class="pack-note">Before boarding the ship Apr 6 — no WiFi or cell service for 4 days. In Safari: share → Add to Home Screen, then open it once while on WiFi so it caches. Or screenshot every tab.</span></label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>🧥 Clothing — Patagonia (Cold, Windy, Wet)</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p1"><label for="p1">Thermal undershirts (2–3)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p2"><label for="p2">Warm mid-layer (fleece or down jacket)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p3"><label for="p3">Waterproof outer shell / rain jacket</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p4"><label for="p4">Waterproof hiking pants or quick-dry pants</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p5"><label for="p5">Buff merino wool neck gaiter ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p6"><label for="p6">Waterproof brimmed hat ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p7"><label for="p7">Warm gloves</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p8"><label for="p8">Moisture-wicking hiking socks (multiple pairs)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p9"><label for="p9">Waterproof hiking boots (broken in!)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p10"><label for="p10">Casual clothes for Buenos Aires (80s, more dressed)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p11"><label for="p11">Light layers for Iguassu (hot & humid, 90°F+)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p11b"><label for="p11b">Swimsuit <span class="pack-note">Amerian Portal del Iguazu has a pool — you'll want it after jungle hikes</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p12"><label for="p12">Permethrin-treated clothes set aside for Brazil leg ✓</label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>🎒 Gear</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p13"><label for="p13">Trekking poles ✓ <span class="pack-note">Use on all descents — knees take a beating on steep rocky trails. Adjust length: shorter going up, longer going down.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p14"><label for="p14">Trekking pole backpack attachment straps ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p15"><label for="p15">Sunglasses ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p16"><label for="p16">Festty eyewear retainer (keeps sunglasses on in wind/Zodiacs) ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p17"><label for="p17">Daypack / small backpack for excursions</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p18"><label for="p18">Waterproof backpack ✓ <span class="pack-note">Use for Zodiac landings — everything inside stays dry</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p19"><label for="p19">Sea to Summit travel clothes line ✓ <span class="pack-note">String between fixtures in your hotel room to dry handwashed socks, base layers. Ship cabin and small hotel rooms — this is how you avoid overpacking clothes.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p20"><label for="p20">Laundry detergent sheets ✓ <span class="pack-note">Use with the clothes line — wash base layers and socks in the sink overnight</span></label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>🔌 Electronics</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p21"><label for="p21">Epicka world travel adapter (TA-105C) ✓ <span class="pack-note">Argentina/Chile use Type C/I plugs</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p22"><label for="p22">GE UltraPro mini power strip (3 outlets) ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p23"><label for="p23">Phone + charging cable</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p24"><label for="p24">Camera + charger / extra batteries</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p25"><label for="p25">Portable phone battery pack / power bank ✓ <span class="pack-note">Charge fully before each active day — limited outlets on ship</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p26"><label for="p26">Headphones / earbuds</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p26b"><label for="p26b">Eye mask ✓ <span class="pack-note">Packed away — find and set aside before trip</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p26c"><label for="p26c">Travel neck pillow ✓ <span class="pack-note">Packed away — find and set aside before trip</span></label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>🧴 Toiletries & Skincare</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p27"><label for="p27">Sunscreen (strong — high altitude + reflection off ice) <span class="pack-note">SPF 50+ minimum. Apply even on cloudy days — UV is intense at altitude and reflects off glaciers and water. You will burn without feeling hot.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p28"><label for="p28">Lip balm with SPF <span class="pack-note">Wind and UV together destroy lips fast. Apply before every outdoor excursion and reapply constantly — this isn't optional in Patagonia.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p29"><label for="p29">Burt's Bees hand salve ✓ <span class="pack-note">Thicker, waxy formula — use at night or when hands are really dry/cracked. Patagonia wind is brutal on skin.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p30"><label for="p30">O'Keeffe's Working Hands cream ✓ <span class="pack-note">Daily hand moisturizer — apply every morning and after Zodiac landings when hands get wet and cold. Prevents cracking before it starts.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p31"><label for="p31">Hand sanitizer / hand wipes ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p32"><label for="p32">Mosquito repellent (skin) ✓ <span class="pack-note">Mainly for Buenos Aires & Iguassu</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p33"><label for="p33">Magicfiber microfiber cloths (glasses) ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p34"><label for="p34">Standard toiletries (toothbrush, etc.)</label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>🩹 First Aid & Comfort</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p35"><label for="p35">Compeed blister pads x2 packs ✓ <span class="pack-note">Treatment once a blister forms — apply immediately at first hot spot and leave on. Body Glide prevents; Compeed treats. Don't wait until it's a full blister.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p36"><label for="p36">Body Glide Foot Glide anti-blister balm ✓ <span class="pack-note">Apply to heels, toes, and any friction points before every hike — not after blisters form. Think of it like sunscreen: you put it on before you need it.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p37"><label for="p37">Blue traveler's med kit (full kit) ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p38"><label for="p38">All medications (see Medications tab)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p39"><label for="p39">Liquid I.V. electrolyte sticks ✓ <span class="pack-note">Use proactively on hike days, bus days, Iguassu heat, and any time you have diarrhea — cold wind masks dehydration</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p40"><label for="p40">Ginger chews ✓ <span class="pack-note">First line for mild queasiness — use before reaching for Bonine. Also good for upset stomach generally. Keep a few in your pocket on ship days and rough bus days.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p40b"><label for="p40b">Kind bars (×6) <span class="pack-note">Emergency snacks — long bus days, delayed meals, early morning Zodiac excursions</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p40c"><label for="p40c">Band-aids (assorted sizes) <span class="pack-note">For small cuts and scrapes from rocks/brush. Always cover with Neosporin first — no urgent care nearby Days 5–12.</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p40d"><label for="p40d">Neosporin / antibiotic ointment <span class="pack-note">Apply under bandaids on any cuts or scrapes — no urgent care nearby on ship days</span></label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>💊 Daily Medications & Sleep</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="pm1"><label for="pm1">Allegra (fexofenadine) — daily allergy <span class="pack-note">Take every day as usual, don't skip</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="pm2"><label for="pm2">Melatonin <span class="pack-note">JFK→Buenos Aires overnight flight + jet lag adjustment</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="pm3"><label for="pm3">Magnesium glycinate <span class="pack-note">Sleep + muscle recovery — take nightly on high-activity days, not just on the flight</span></label></div>
    </div>
    <div class="pack-category">
    <h3>📄 Documents</h3>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p41"><label for="p41">Passport</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p42"><label for="p42">Brazil e-visa (printed copy) ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p43"><label for="p43">Yellow fever exemption card ✓</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p44"><label for="p44">Emergency document (passport copy + card numbers) — printed, packed separately from passport</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p44b"><label for="p44b">Emergency document saved to phone Notes app <span class="pack-note">Photo of completed doc + key numbers typed out = accessible even offline</span></label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p45"><label for="p45">Travel insurance card / policy number</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p46"><label for="p46">OAT booking confirmation</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p47"><label for="p47">Credit cards (notify banks of travel dates)</label></div>
    <div class="pack-item"><input type="checkbox" id="p48"><label for="p48">USD cash $500 <span class="pack-note">20×$1 (housekeeping), 24×$10 (TEL tip, guides, taxis), 12×$20 (buffer) — all USD preferred for tipping</span></label></div>
    </div>
    </div>
    <!-- MEDICATIONS -->
    <div id="meds" class="section">
    <div class="section-title">Medications</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">What to take and when</div>
    <div class="info-block" style="background:#f8f8f6;border:1px solid #ccc;padding:14px;margin-bottom:18px;">
    <h3>📅 When to Take What — Quick Reference</h3>
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:11px;margin-top:6px;">
    <tr style="background:#eee;"><th style="text-align:left;padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">When</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Take</th></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Every single day</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Allegra (1 tablet)</td></tr>
    <tr style="background:#f5f5f5;"><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Apr 2 — overnight flight (JFK)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Melatonin + Magnesium glycinate (30min before sleep)</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Apr 5 evening (night before ship)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">⚠️ Bonine — START HERE, don't wait</td></tr>
    <tr style="background:#f5f5f5;"><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Apr 6–9 (ship days)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Bonine nightly · Ginger chews as needed · Escalate to Dramamine if needed</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Big hike days (Apr 6, 7, 10, 11)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Ibuprofen if sore · Liquid I.V. · Magnesium glycinate at night for recovery</td></tr>
    <tr style="background:#f5f5f5;"><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Long bus day (Apr 13)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Liquid I.V. · Kind bar · Ibuprofen if needed</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>If diarrhea starts</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Hydrate with Trioral salts first — let mild cases run their course. Imodium only if disruptive (long bus, can't miss activity). Azithromycin only if fever/6+ stools/blood.</td></tr>
    <tr style="background:#f5f5f5;"><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;"><strong>Iguassu (Apr 17–18, hot & humid)</strong></td><td style="padding:5px 8px;border:1px solid #ccc;">Liquid I.V. · Mosquito repellent daily · Wear permethrin-treated clothes</td></tr>
    </table>
    </div>
    <div class="seasick-ladder">
    <h3>🚢 Seasickness Ladder — Start Gentle, Escalate if Needed</h3>
    <div class="ladder-step"><div class="ladder-num">1</div><div><strong>Ginger chews</strong> — anytime, no side effects, good for mild queasiness</div></div>
    <div class="ladder-step"><div class="ladder-num">2</div><div><strong>Bonine</strong> (meclizine, non-drowsy) — take the night before boarding (Apr 5 evening) and continue as needed</div></div>
    <div class="ladder-step"><div class="ladder-num">3</div><div><strong>Dramamine Original</strong> (drowsy) — step up if Bonine isn't enough; plan around it causing sleepiness</div></div>
    <div class="ladder-step"><div class="ladder-num">4</div><div><strong>Dramamine Naturals</strong> (ginger-based) — gentlest pharmaceutical option, good daytime backup</div></div>
    <p style="font-size:10px;color:var(--muted);margin-top:8px;">⚠️ Roughest conditions: Cape Horn (Day 6) and Strait of Magellan. Days 5–8 are all at sea.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Bonine (Meclizine)</div>
    <span class="med-type seasick">Seasickness · Non-Drowsy</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: Night of Apr 5 (before boarding Apr 6), continue nightly</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Non-drowsy antihistamine. Take once daily. Works best when taken preventively — don't wait until you feel sick.</div>
    <div class="med-note">💡 This is your primary seasickness defense. Start it the evening before you board.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Dramamine Original</div>
    <span class="med-type seasick">Seasickness · Drowsy</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: If Bonine alone isn't working; plan for drowsiness</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Stronger than Bonine but causes significant drowsiness. Don't combine with alcohol. Best taken when you can rest.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Dramamine Naturals</div>
    <span class="med-type seasick">Seasickness · Ginger-Based</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: As gentler daytime alternative or supplement</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Ginger-based, minimal side effects. Can be used alongside or instead of Bonine on milder sea days.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Ibuprofen</div>
    <span class="med-type pain">Pain / Anti-Inflammatory</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: As needed for muscle soreness, headache, inflammation</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Standard dosing. Good for next-day muscle soreness after demanding hikes (Days 6, 7, 10, 11). Take with food.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Imodium (Loperamide)</div>
    <span class="med-type gi">GI · Anti-Diarrheal</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: Only when diarrhea is disruptive — NOT for mild or early cases</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Slows gut motility — which means it stops your body from flushing out whatever is causing the problem. For mild diarrhea, let it run its course and focus on hydration (Trioral salts + Liquid I.V.). Use Imodium when: you have a long bus ride or active excursion you cannot miss, OR diarrhea is frequent enough to be debilitating. Follow package dosing.</div>
    <div class="med-note">⚠️ Never use if you have fever or bloody stool — that means infection, and blocking it in is dangerous. Use Azithromycin instead and seek care.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Azithromycin (Z-Pack)</div>
    <span class="med-type antibiotic">Antibiotic · Prescription</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: Only if severe traveler's diarrhea (6+ loose stools/day, fever, or blood)</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Broad-spectrum antibiotic prescribed by travel clinic. This is your "big gun" — don't use for mild GI upset. Complete the full course if you start it.</div>
    <div class="med-note">⚠️ Save this for genuine need. Consult a doctor if possible before starting. Remote locations means no nearby medical facilities Days 5–12.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Blue Travel Kit</div>
    <span class="med-type kit">Travel Clinic Kit</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Use: As needed throughout trip</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Contents: AssureGI (digestive protection), DiaResQ (diarrhea relief), Imodium, water purification tablets, Trioral rehydration salts, Pepto Bismol, Tums, laxatives, measuring syringe, antiseptic towelettes.</div>
    <div class="med-note">💡 Trioral rehydration salts — use if you have diarrhea or are very dehydrated. Mix with clean water.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Allegra (Fexofenadine)</div>
    <span class="med-type pain">Daily · Allergy</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: Every day, same as home — don't skip</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Your daily antihistamine. Continue throughout entire trip including travel days. Patagonia has strong winds that kick up dust and pollen; Buenos Aires is a major city with urban allergens.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Melatonin</div>
    <span class="med-type kit">Sleep · JFK → Buenos Aires Flight</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: ~30 min before you want to fall asleep on the plane</div>
    <div class="med-detail">JFK to Buenos Aires is roughly 11–12 hours overnight. Take melatonin once you're settled and the cabin lights are dimmed. Standard dose 0.5–3mg is usually enough — more isn't better. Also use for jet lag adjustment first 1–2 nights.</div>
    <div class="med-note">💡 Pair with eye mask + neck pillow. Don't look at your phone screen after taking it. Buenos Aires is only 1 hour ahead of ET so jet lag will be minimal.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Magnesium Glycinate</div>
    <span class="med-type kit">Sleep Support</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Take: With melatonin on flight, and on restless nights during trip</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Magnesium glycinate is well-absorbed and calming without side effects. Works synergistically with melatonin — the combination is more effective than either alone for sleep onset. Also helpful for muscle recovery after demanding hikes.</div>
    <div class="med-note">💡 On high-activity days (Days 6, 7, 10, 11), taking MG glycinate at night can help with both sleep quality and next-day muscle soreness.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="med-card">
    <div class="med-name">Liquid I.V. Electrolyte Sticks</div>
    <span class="med-type gi">Hydration</span>
    <div class="med-timing">⏰ Use: Hike days, bus days, Iguassu heat, and any time you have diarrhea</div>
    <div class="med-detail">Mix one stick into 16oz water. Not just for emergencies — use proactively on any high-output day. Key moments: every major hike day (Days 6, 7, 10, 11, 12), the 10–12hr bus day (Day 13), and all of Iguassu (Days 16–18) where heat and humidity will drain you fast. Also use if you have diarrhea — rehydration is the first priority before anything else.</div>
    <div class="med-note">💡 The cold and wind in Patagonia masks how much you're sweating and losing fluids. You may not feel thirsty but you're still depleting electrolytes on long hikes.</div>
    </div>
    </div>
    <!-- NEED TO KNOW -->
    <div id="need-to-know" class="section">
    <div class="section-title">Need to Know</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">Key info for the trip</div>
    <div class="alert-block">
    <strong>⚠️ Action Required Before Departure</strong>
    Brazil e-visa required — must bring printed copy. Arrival date: approx Apr 17. Period of stay: 1 day.
    </div>
    <div class="alert-block">
    <strong>⚠️ Permethrin Clothes Treatment</strong>
    Treat clothes for Brazil (Iguassu) leg with Permethrin spray 24–48hrs before departure. Let dry fully. This protects against mosquitoes carrying dengue and yellow fever. You have the yellow fever exemption card but treatment still recommended.
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>✈️ JFK → Buenos Aires Flight Sleep Strategy</h3>
    <p>~11–12 hour overnight flight. Buenos Aires is only 1hr ahead of ET so jet lag will be minimal on arrival — the main goal is just arriving rested.</p>
    <ul style="margin-top:8px;">
    <li>Take melatonin (0.5–3mg) + magnesium glycinate ~30min before you want to sleep</li>
    <li>Eye mask + neck pillow matter more than anything — bring both in your carry-on</li>
    <li>Avoid phone screen after taking melatonin</li>
    <li>Eat light on the plane — heavy meals make sleep harder</li>
    <li>Limit alcohol — dehydrating at altitude and disrupts sleep quality</li>
    <li>Ask for a window seat if possible — something to lean against</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🚢 Managing Seasickness — Practical Tips</h3>
    <ul>
    <li><strong>Start Bonine the night before boarding (Apr 5)</strong> — don't wait until you feel sick</li>
    <li>Stay on deck in fresh air — going below to a closed cabin makes it much worse</li>
    <li>Keep your eyes on the horizon, not on your phone or a book</li>
    <li>Eat light but don't be on an empty stomach — crackers, bread work well</li>
    <li>Midship and lower decks have the least motion</li>
    <li>Ginger chews work surprisingly well as a first-line option</li>
    <li>The crew does this route constantly — they know exactly what's coming</li>
    <li>Cape Horn landings are weather-dependent — they won't send you if it's unsafe</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🌡️ Weather by Destination</h3>
    <p>Patagonia (Days 5–13): 50–60°F, wind up to 44mph with gusts to 56mph, rain possible anytime. Layer aggressively — it changes fast.<br><br>
    Buenos Aires (Days 2–3, 14–15): Daily highs in the 80s. Much warmer — pack a separate layer set.<br><br>
    Iguassu Falls (Days 16–18): 90°F+ with 90% humidity. Completely different climate. You'll get wet from the falls — bring a dry bag for your phone.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🚢 Life on the Ventus Australis (Days 5–8)</h3>
    <ul>
    <li>210-passenger small ship — not a massive cruise liner</li>
    <li>All excursions via Zodiac inflatable boats — requires balance and agility to board</li>
    <li>Shore landings are weather-dependent — Cape Horn especially may be skipped</li>
    <li>No elevator on ship</li>
    <li>Attire always casual — no need to dress up for dinner</li>
    <li>Dinners start late by American standards</li>
    <li>Complimentary beverages available throughout voyage</li>
    <li>Lecture series on glaciology, history, wildlife in ship's lounge</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🥾 Physical Demands</h3>
    <ul>
    <li>Must walk 3 miles unassisted on rough terrain, 6–8 hrs activity/day</li>
    <li>5 treks of ~2hrs each on steep, uneven, rocky, often muddy terrain</li>
    <li>Cape Horn: 160 steps up + 2-mile walk</li>
    <li>Wulaia Bay: 3-mile uphill walk</li>
    <li>Day 12 (Torres del Paine to El Calafate): demanding afternoon hike + 10–12hr bus</li>
    <li>No medical facilities nearby Days 5–12</li>
    <li>Horseback riding Day 10 (~1hr)</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>💳 Money & Payments</h3>
    <ul>
    <li>Optional tours paid by credit card on trip (Visa, MC, Discover only — no Amex)</li>
    <li>Optional tour charges may post 2–3 months after return — use a card that won't expire</li>
    <li>La Tienda de Vinos wine tasting: $41/person (Day 13)</li>
    <li>Great Adventure boat tour: $75/person (Day 18)</li>
    <li>Notify your bank of travel dates before departure</li>
    <li><strong>Bring ~$500 USD cash:</strong> 20× $1 (housekeeping), 24× $10 (TEL tip, guides, taxis), 12× $20 (buffer) — all USD, small bills preferred</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🐧 Wildlife You May See</h3>
    <p>Magellanic penguins (120,000+ at Magdalena Island) · Andean condors · Guanaco (llama relative) · Puma · Patagonian gray fox · Sea lions (Marta Island) · Crested cara cara · Coatis at Iguassu · Hummingbirds at Iguassu hotel</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📋 Visas Summary</h3>
    <ul>
    <li><strong>Argentina:</strong> No visa needed for U.S. citizens ✓</li>
    <li><strong>Chile:</strong> No visa needed for U.S. citizens ✓</li>
    <li><strong>Brazil:</strong> E-visa required — must apply in advance, bring printed copy ⚠️</li>
    </ul>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📞 Key Contacts</h3>
    <p>OAT Emergency: 1-800-955-1925<br>
    PVS International (visa help): 1-800-556-9990<br>
    Brazilian Embassy DC: (202) 238-2700<br>
    Brazil e-visa website: brazil.vfsevisa.com</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block" style="border-left: 4px solid #c0392b;">
    <h3>🏥 Travel Insurance — TripMate Plan UF694B</h3>
    <p><strong>Plan Number:</strong> UF694B &nbsp;|&nbsp; <strong>Provider:</strong> TripMate / Generali Global Assistance</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>🚨 Emergency assistance (outside US, call collect):</strong><br>
    <span style="font-size:15px;font-weight:bold;">+1-954-334-8953</span></p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Toll-free (US/Canada):</strong> 1-866-260-0362<br>
    <strong>Email:</strong> <a href="/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection" class="__cf_email__" data-cfemail="1877686b587f7f79356d6b79367b7775">[email&#160;protected]</a><br>
    <strong>Claims:</strong> 1-866-900-8452 · travelclaimsonline.com</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>What's covered:</strong> Emergency medical evacuation $75K · Trip interruption 150% of trip cost · Medical/dental $25K · Baggage loss $1,600 · Trip delay (8hr+) $500 · Baggage delay (24hr+) $400</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>If a bag is lost:</strong> (1) Report to airline, get file reference number. (2) Report to Blue Ribbon Bags within 24 hours: tripmate.blueribbonbags.com or call 888-BAGGAGE.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>To file a claim:</strong> Report within 20 days of loss. Online at travelclaimsonline.com or call 1-866-900-8452.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🦶 FootprintID — Medical Records Card</h3>
    <p>Your travel insurance includes a service that stores your medical info (medications, allergies, conditions) so local doctors anywhere in the world can access it in an emergency.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Membership #:</strong> 80-331-751 &nbsp;|&nbsp; <strong>Access Code:</strong> 1183<br>
    <strong>Emergency line:</strong> +1-855-374-3411<br>
    <strong>Register/login:</strong> tripmate.footprintid.com</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Card:</strong> Keep the blue FootprintID card in your wallet throughout the trip. If you're incapacitated, emergency responders can call the number on the card to access your health information.</p>
    </div>
    </div>
    <div id="culture" class="section">
    <div class="section-title">Culture & Places</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">What to know · What to see · Where to go</div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🇦🇷 Argentina — Cultural Basics</h3>
    <p><strong>Greetings:</strong> A single kiss on the right cheek is standard for hello and goodbye — even with strangers in social settings. Men typically shake hands with each other. Don't be surprised if it happens immediately upon meeting someone.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Time:</strong> Argentines run late — socially, 30+ minutes late is normal and expected. Dinner doesn't start until 9 or 10pm. Don't be the first to arrive at anything social.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Mate:</strong> If someone offers you mate (the national drink — a bitter herbal tea sipped through a metal straw from a shared gourd), accepting is a gesture of friendship. Pass it back to the person in charge after drinking — they refill it for everyone. Only say "gracias" if you're done and don't want more.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Food culture:</strong> Argentine beef is world-class — order it. Don't ask for anything well done. Food is generally mild, not spicy. Dulce de leche (caramel-like spread) is everywhere and beloved — don't compare it to caramel.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Safety:</strong> Buenos Aires is safe for tourists with normal urban awareness. Keep your bag in front of you at cafés, don't flash expensive electronics, and be alert in crowded markets and bus stations. Recoleta is one of the safest neighborhoods.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Tipping:</strong> 10% in restaurants is standard and appreciated. Leave cash. Taxis — round up the fare. Not expected but welcome.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Thumbs up:</strong> Can be considered rude — use a wave or nod instead.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Sensitive topics:</strong> The Perón era and the Malvinas (Falkland Islands) are emotionally charged for Argentines. Avoid bringing them up. Politics and the economy are things Argentines love to discuss among themselves — not so much with foreign guests.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Ordering steak:</strong> If you don't specify, it comes medium to well done. Rare is genuinely hard to get here. Also, Argentines salt everything heavily — if you want less salt, say "no use sal, por favor."</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Photography:</strong> Always ask before taking close-up photos of people. Do not photograph military personnel, police, or official buildings — your camera can be confiscated.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>💵 Money — Critical Tips from OAT</h3>
    <p><strong>Credit cards in Argentina:</strong> Visa is commonly accepted; MasterCard and Amex often are not. Some businesses add a 5–10% surcharge for card use (look for the word "recargo"). You cannot leave a tip on a credit card — always have cash for tips.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Currency exchange:</strong> Your Trip Experience Leader will guide you to an exchange office for the best rate. Bring large, crisp, undamaged U.S. bills — ideally $100 bills post-2013. The rate is significantly better for $100s than $20s. Never exchange on the street.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>ATMs:</strong> PIN must be numeric only — many foreign ATMs have no letters on the keypad. ATMs may only accept local bank cards. Have a backup card.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Chile:</strong> Credit cards add a 2–4% surcharge. Generally not accepted in remote locations.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🍽️ OAT Tipping Guide</h3>
    <p><strong>Trip Experience Leader:</strong> $10–14 USD per person per day they're with you. Cash only. Budget this out before you go.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Hotel housekeeping:</strong> $1–2 per room per night. Leave daily, not at checkout.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Restaurants:</strong> 10% cash tip expected in Argentina and Chile. Cannot be added to a credit card.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Taxis:</strong> Round up the fare — not required but common.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Already included in trip price:</strong> Tips for local guides, drivers, ship crew, and luggage porters. No need to tip these separately.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🌿 Chile — Key Notes</h3>
    <p><strong>Pinochet era:</strong> Chile still feels the aftershocks of the brutal 1973–1990 military dictatorship. Sensitive topic — follow your guide's lead if it comes up.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Food to try:</strong> Centolla (king crab) in Punta Arenas. Empanadas. Pisco sour — the national cocktail. Note: coffee in restaurants is usually instant; find a café for real coffee.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>⚠️ Agricultural warning entering Chile:</strong> Chile strictly prohibits bringing in any fruit, nuts, vegetables, seeds, dairy, flowers, or unprocessed meats. Fines up to $200 even for a single apple or pack of peanuts. Declare or discard before landing.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>⚠️ No WiFi on the ship:</strong> The Ventus Australis has no internet. Satellite phone available for emergencies (~$3/min). Download maps, books, and anything else you need before boarding in Ushuaia.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📍 Buenos Aires — Recoleta Neighborhood</h3>
    <p>Your hotel (Recoleta Grand) is in one of the city's most walkable, elegant neighborhoods — often called the "Paris of South America." French-style architecture, wide tree-lined boulevards, outdoor cafés, museums, and parks all within easy walking distance.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Recoleta Cemetery</strong> — 5 min walk. One of the world's great cemeteries — more like an open-air museum with elaborate mausoleums the size of small chapels. Eva Perón is buried here. Allow 1–2 hours. Free entry. English guided tours Tue/Thu at 11am.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>El Ateneo Grand Splendid</strong> — 15 min walk. A working bookstore inside a stunning 1919 theater — the stage is a café, the balconies are browsing areas. One of the most beautiful bookstores in the world. Worth a visit even if you don't buy anything.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Floralis Genérica</strong> — 10 min walk. A giant steel flower sculpture in a reflecting pool. Its petals open at dawn and close at dusk. Oddly moving.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>National Museum of Fine Arts (MNBA)</strong> — 10 min walk. Free. European and Argentine works spanning 800+ years. Good option on a hot afternoon.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>La Biela Café</strong> — right next to the cemetery. Historic café where motor racing champions gathered in the 1950s. Great spot for coffee and people-watching under a massive rubber tree.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Plaza Francia weekend market</strong> — if you're there on a weekend, there's an artisan fair in the plaza adjacent to the cemetery. Crafts, art, street performers.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Palermo</strong> — 15 min by taxi. Hipper neighborhood with the best restaurant scene, parks, and nightlife. Good dinner destination in the evenings.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📍 Ushuaia — End of the World</h3>
    <p>The southernmost city in the world, with a dramatic mountain backdrop dropping straight into the Beagle Channel. More of a base than a destination, but worth a wander.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Museo del Fin del Mundo</strong> — Small but genuinely interesting museum covering the history of Tierra del Fuego, indigenous Yámana people, and early European explorers. Good rainy afternoon option.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Main street (San Martín)</strong> — The pedestrian strip has shops, restaurants, and the tourist vibe is fun to absorb. King crab (centolla) is the local specialty — worth trying at least once.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Beagle Channel views</strong> — You'll get plenty of this on the ship, but walking along the waterfront before boarding is a nice way to absorb where you are before departure.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📍 Puerto Natales — Gateway to Torres del Paine</h3>
    <p>Small, unpretentious Chilean town that exists largely to serve trekkers headed to Torres del Paine. Charming in its own right — worth a couple hours of wandering.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Waterfront (costanera)</strong> — Walk along the Last Hope Sound with views of the mountains. Especially beautiful at dusk.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Baguales Brewery</strong> — Puerto Natales' first microbrewery. Great place to have a pint and talk to fellow travelers about their experiences in the park.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Milodón Cave</strong> — 25km out of town. Where a piece of ancient giant ground sloth skin was discovered in the 1890s. Interesting historical site if you have transport.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>📍 Punta Arenas — Strait of Magellan</h3>
    <p>Your ship embarkation point. Historic port city at the southern tip of South America — once a vital stop on the clipper route before the Panama Canal made it redundant.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Plaza Muñoz Gamero</strong> — The main square with a monument to Magellan. Local tradition: kiss the foot of the indigenous figure on the statue for good luck.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Cementerio Municipal</strong> — Like Recoleta but wilder and more atmospheric. The elaborate tombs of the city's founding families are fascinating.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Museo Regional de Magallanes</strong> — Covers the history of the Strait of Magellan, Patagonian settlement, and indigenous cultures. Worth an hour.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🌊 On the Ship — Cultural Notes</h3>
    <p><strong>Yámana people:</strong> The indigenous people of Tierra del Fuego who survived in these extreme conditions for thousands of years — wearing almost no clothing despite the cold, living in canoes. Wulaia Bay was one of their largest settlements. The small museum there is worth your time.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Cape Horn history:</strong> Before the Panama Canal, every ship traveling between the Atlantic and Pacific had to round the Horn. Thousands of sailors died here. Standing on the island puts you at one of the most historically significant maritime locations on earth.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Darwin connection:</strong> The Beagle Channel is named for the ship Charles Darwin sailed on during his 1831–1836 voyage. He described these exact waters and the Yámana people in detail. Walking through Wulaia Bay is walking in Darwin's footsteps.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🇧🇷 Iguassu Falls — Brazil Side</h3>
    <p>The Brazilian side of the falls gives the panoramic view — you see the full scale of the horseshoe. The Argentine side (if your tour includes it) lets you get right up to and partially inside the falls. Both are extraordinary.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Devil's Throat (Garganta del Diablo):</strong> The main event — a massive U-shaped waterfall where the roar is physical. You will get wet. Bring the waterproof shell.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Wildlife:</strong> Coatis (raccoon-like animals) are everywhere and will try to steal your food. Don't feed them. Toucans, butterflies the size of your hand, and capybaras are all possible.</p>
    <br>
    <p><strong>Heat:</strong> 90°F+ with high humidity. Start early, stay hydrated (Liquid I.V. days), wear permethrin clothes and repellent from the moment you step outside.</p>
    </div>
    </div>
    <!-- ROUTE MAP -->
    <div id="route-map" class="section">
    <div class="section-title">Route Map</div>
    <div class="section-subtitle">OAT official itinerary map</div>
    <div style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:4px;overflow:hidden;margin-bottom:20px;background:white;">
    <img src="route-map.png"
    alt="OAT Patagonia Route Map"
    style="width:100%;height:auto;display:block;" />
    </div>
    <div class="info-block">
    <h3>🗺️ Reading the Map</h3>
    <p>The dashed red line is the air route; the solid blue line is the cruise/ferry route aboard the M/V Ventus Australis. The main map covers southern Chile and Argentina — the inset (top right) shows the post-trip Iguassu Falls extension near the Brazil/Argentina/Paraguay border.</p>
    <br>
    <p>Key waypoints south to north: <strong>Ushuaia → Cape Horn → Wulaia Bay → Glacier Alley → Pia Glacier → Ainsworth Bay → Tuckers Islets → Punta Arenas</strong>, then overland to Puerto Natales → Torres del Paine → El Calafate → Buenos Aires → Iguassu Falls.</p>
    </div>
    </div>
    <script>
    function showTab(id) {
    document.querySelectorAll('.section').forEach(s => s.classList.remove('active'));
    document.querySelectorAll('.tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));
    document.getElementById(id).classList.add('active');
    event.target.classList.add('active');
    }
    // Countdown
    function updateCountdown() {
    const departure = new Date('2026-04-02T00:00:00');
    const now = new Date();
    const diff = Math.ceil((departure - now) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
    const el = document.getElementById('countdown');
    if (diff > 0) el.textContent = '🧭 ' + diff + ' days to departure';
    else if (diff === 0) el.textContent = '✈️ Departure day!';
    else el.textContent = '🌍 Trip in progress!';
    }
    updateCountdown();
    // Packing progress
    function updateProgress() {
    const all = document.querySelectorAll('#packing input[type="checkbox"]');
    const checked = document.querySelectorAll('#packing input[type="checkbox"]:checked');
    const pct = all.length ? Math.round((checked.length / all.length) * 100) : 0;
    document.getElementById('pack-bar').style.width = pct + '%';
    document.getElementById('pack-count').textContent = checked.length + ' of ' + all.length + ' packed (' + pct + '%)';
    }
    // Save checkbox state to localStorage
    function saveCheckboxes() {
    const state = {};
    document.querySelectorAll('#packing input[type="checkbox"]').forEach(cb => {
    state[cb.id] = cb.checked;
    });
    localStorage.setItem('patagonia-packing', JSON.stringify(state));
    }
    // Restore checkbox state from localStorage
    function restoreCheckboxes() {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem('patagonia-packing');
    if (!saved) return;
    const state = JSON.parse(saved);
    document.querySelectorAll('#packing input[type="checkbox"]').forEach(cb => {
    if (cb.id in state) cb.checked = state[cb.id];
    });
    }
    restoreCheckboxes();
    updateProgress();
    document.querySelectorAll('#packing input[type="checkbox"]').forEach(cb => {
    cb.addEventListener('change', () => {
    saveCheckboxes();
    updateProgress();
    });
    });
    </script>
    </body>
    </html>
